L1.area: Internal data serialization and distributed system communication infrastructure

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where data received over a network port is deserialized using Java's standard object deserialization mechanism without any class filtering or whitelist validation. An unprivileged user with network access to the affected service can craft malicious serialized objects that, when deserialized, lead to remote code execution if suitable gadget classes are present on the classpath.

L3.area: TCP-based locator server and its core data serialization handling for cluster member communication
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the locator's TCP server, which opens a network port that deserializes incoming data using Java's standard object deserialization mechanism without any class whitelist or blacklist filtering. An attacker with network access to the locator port can supply crafted serialized objects that, upon deserialization, trigger arbitrary code execution if suitable gadget classes exist on the classpath.

ground_truth.reason: The TcpServer in the Geode locator deserializes data from a network port using Java ObjectInputStream without any serialization filter, allowing an attacker to instantiate arbitrary classes and achieve remote code execution.
